g.text: Shared API vs Backend-for-Frontend — same four clients, two response strategies
Drama: one fat 12 KB response is sent to every client (left) vs. four tailored responses fitted per client (right).
Same response shape for every client; over-fetch on small clients;
Pain: every schema change must be reviewed by every consumer team.
Bottleneck is cross-team coordination, not bandwidth.
Each client gets its own backend, owned by the same team.
Win: each team ships independently. BFFs are TRANSLATION ONLY — never own business logic.
Anchor: Watch payload shrinks 12 KB → 0.5 KB (24×). Mobile shrinks 12 KB → 2 KB (6×). Web stays ~the same but is no longer coupled to mobile.
